edges.description: Enzyme deficiency leads to lysosomal accumulation of undegraded glycoconjugates.
Multisystem lysosomal storage produces the intermediate type 3 NAGA-deficiency phenotype.
Neuronal lysosomal storage contributes to epilepsy in the intermediate NAGA-deficiency phenotype.
Neuronal lysosomal storage contributes to behavioral abnormalities.
Neuronal lysosomal storage contributes to autistic neurodevelopmental features in type 3 disease.
Neuronal lysosomal storage contributes to impaired psychomotor development.
Neuronal lysosomal storage contributes to impaired cognitive development.
Multisystem lysosomal storage can include developmental cataract.
Multisystem lysosomal storage can include ocular motor abnormalities.
Multisystem lysosomal storage can involve cardiac muscle.
Visceral lysosomal storage can enlarge the liver.

nodes.description: Biallelic NAGA variants abolish lysosomal alpha-N-acetylgalactosaminidase, the hydrolase that removes terminal alpha-N-acetylgalactosamine residues from glycopeptides and glycolipids.

evidence.reference_title: Structural and immunocytochemical studies on alpha-N-acetylgalactosaminidase deficiency (Schindler/Kanzaki disease).
evidence.snippet: Alpha-N-acetylgalactosaminidase (alpha-NAGA) deficiency (Schindler/Kanzaki disease) is a clinically and pathologically heterogeneous genetic disease with a wide spectrum including an early onset neuroaxonal dystrophy (Schindler disease) and late onset angiokeratoma corporis diffusum (Kanzaki disease)

nodes.description: Autism is reported by Orphanet as a very frequent neurodevelopmental feature of NAGA deficiency type 3.
Behavioral abnormalities in the intermediate group.

evidence.reference_title: Human alpha-N-acetylgalactosaminidase (alpha-NAGA) deficiency: no association with neuroaxonal dystrophy?
evidence.snippet: a very heterogeneous group of patients with various degrees of epilepsy/behavioural difficulties/psychomotor retardation

nodes.description: Congenital cataract reported in an intermediate-form index patient.

evidence.reference_title: Human alpha-N-acetylgalactosaminidase (alpha-NAGA) deficiency: no association with neuroaxonal dystrophy?
evidence.snippet: has congenital cataract, slight motor retardation and secondary demyelinisation

evidence.reference_title: Human alpha-N-acetylgalactosaminidase (alpha-NAGA) deficiency: no association with neuroaxonal dystrophy?
evidence.snippet: suggest that factors or genes other than alpha-NAGA contribute to the clinical heterogeneity

nodes.description: Liver enlargement reported in the type 3 NAGA-deficiency phenotype spectrum.
Cardiac hypertrophic involvement reported in the type 3 NAGA-deficiency phenotype spectrum.
Intellectual disability is reported as a very frequent feature of the NAGA deficiency type 3 phenotype.
Type 3 is classically an intermediate phenotype between the severe infantile (type 1) and mild adult (type 2) forms.

evidence.reference_title: A New Case of Schindler Disease.
evidence.snippet: Schindler disease type III shows an intermediate phenotype between types I and II

nodes.description: Failure to cleave terminal alpha-N-acetylgalactosamine causes lysosomal accumulation of incompletely degraded glycoconjugates and cytoplasmic vacuolisation across multiple cell types, driving the intermediate-severity multisystem phenotype.

evidence.reference_title: Human alpha-N-acetylgalactosaminidase (alpha-NAGA) deficiency: no association with neuroaxonal dystrophy?
evidence.snippet: angiokeratoma and clear vacuolisation in various cell types

evidence.reference_title: Human alpha-N-acetylgalactosaminidase (alpha-NAGA) deficiency: no association with neuroaxonal dystrophy?
evidence.snippet: Both sibs are homozygous for the E325K mutation, the same genotype that was found in the most severe form of alpha-NAGA deficiency

evidence.reference_title: Human alpha-N-acetylgalactosaminidase (alpha-NAGA) deficiency: no association with neuroaxonal dystrophy?
evidence.snippet: a very heterogeneous group of patients with various degrees of epilepsy/behavioural difficulties/psychomotor retardation

nodes.description: Epilepsy of variable degree in the intermediate group.

evidence.reference_title: Human alpha-N-acetylgalactosaminidase (alpha-NAGA) deficiency: no association with neuroaxonal dystrophy?
evidence.snippet: a very heterogeneous group of patients with various degrees of epilepsy/behavioural difficulties/psychomotor retardation

nodes.description: Ocular misalignment reported as a very frequent feature in Orphanet for NAGA deficiency type 3.
